observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management, through the City Accountant and the City Budget Officer, to submit an explanation letter and a detailed breakdown of the material items as reflected in the Reconciliation between actual amounts presented in the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts and the Statement of Financial Performance for the year ended December 31, 2024.
In addition, we strongly advised the management to ensure that succeeding submissions of the SCBAA and its corresponding reconciliation should include detailed disclosures and a breakdown of the material items in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We reiterated our recommendation to the management to direct the:
City Accounting Office to:
Adhere to the recording requirements of the NGAS for the Real Property and Special Education Tax, especially on the basis of setting up the total RPT Receivables at the beginning of the year;
Continue reconciling the accounting records with the CTO records and make the necessary adjustments to correct the balance of the Real Property Tax Receivable, Special Education Tax Receivable, Deferred Real Property and Special Education Tax Income and submit to the audit team the approved JEV together with its supporting documents including the working papers;
Continue the use of the subsidiary ledger/schedule per Barangay and exclude interest, penalties and special levy for idle lands; and
Thereafter, conduct periodic reconciliation of records with the CTO for fair presentation of account balances.
City Treasurer’s Office to:
Continue providing a complete list of taxpayers and the corresponding amount of uncollected delinquent taxes to the CAO necessary for the establishment of the subsidiary ledgers;
Provide accurate and reliable amount of RPT/SET collectibles at the beginning of each year in accordance with Section 20 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (NGAS); and
Continue undertaking periodic reconciliation of records with the City Accounting Office to enable adjustments in the appropriate records for any noted discrepancies.
City Assessor’s Office to:
Assess and conduct inspection of all the noted real properties and equipment in Annex H and all other properties with doubtful information in accordance with existing assessment rules and regulation in order to generate more income for the City; and
Apply procedures necessary for the cancellation of assessments for properties that were already demolished or equipment removed.

We recommended to the management to instruct the CEEDMO to provide the City Accounting Office of the subject list of unpaid rentals by market stallholders as of December 31, 2024 for proper recognition of the receivable and income derived from market operations. Henceforth, CEEDMO is reminded to regularly submit the said data to the City Accounting Office for proper recording in the books.

We recommended to the management to:
Instruct the Inventory Committee and the City Accounting Office to conduct an investigation on the reported variance to reconcile the balances of their records. Henceforth conduct a regular reconciliation of the Inventory accounts;
Instruct the Inventory Committee to gather data on those items in the RPCI with incomplete details for proper reconciliation of balances per ledger card and on hand per count; and
Require the City Accounting Office to draw a JEV for the reclassification of Semi-expendable properties that were already issued to end users and henceforth use the appropriate Semi-expendable Inventory accounts for future acquisitions.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to instruct the City Accountant to:
Reclassify the remaining balance of collection of sale of bidding documents to the account “Trust Liabilities” and use the account to record such collection thereafter; and
Explain why there was no recognition for the transfer of the excess of the collection as part of the local government funds.

We recommended that Management instruct the City Accountant to:
Assign additional personnel for the scanning of disbursement vouchers and the supporting documents; and
Prospectively, ensure that the documents, reports and supporting documents should be submitted within the prescribed period as required under COA Circular Nos. 95-006 and 2009-006.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive shall require the concerned offices to:
Ensure that only PPAs related to DRRM shall be charged to the 5% DRRM Fund of the City and charge to regular funds those disbursements that pertain to the basic services rendered by the LGU; and
Invest in more effective, science-based, long-term, climate-responsive, and disaster-resilient infrastructures, projects, programs, and activities to prepare the Barangays of the City of Malaybalay to adapt to and mitigate the effects of disasters, particularly those brought by climate change.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to instruct the City Accountant and the BAC Secretariat to:
Submit the lacking documentary requirements for the paid BAC honoraria and to observed the completeness of the prescribed supporting documents for every payment of the claim thereafter; and
Ensure that the payrolls are duly signed by the signatories.

Recommendation/s:
We recommended to the management to instruct the City Accounting and City Budget Office to work together in the preparation of the report of reconciliation between the budget and actual amounts and immediately submit them for review, duly supported with the necessary schedules to substantiate the identified reconciling items; and
In the ensuing years, observe the timely submission of the reconciliation reports and its supporting documents and make proper disclosures and explanation in the Notes to Financial Statements, relative to the basis in the preparation of the SCBAA and the reasons for material discrepancies between the budget and actual amounts and other relevant disclosures required in IPSAS 24.

Recommendation/s:
We reiterated our recommendation to the management to direct the:
City Accounting Office to:
Adhere to the recording requirements of the NGAS for the Real Property and Special Education Tax, especially on the basis of setting up the total RPT Receivables at the beginning of the year;
Continue reconciling the accounting records with the CTO records and make the necessary adjustments to correct the balance of the Real Property Tax Receivable, Special Education Tax Receivable, Deferred Real Property and Special Education Tax Income and submit to the audit team the approved JEV together with its supporting documents including the working papers;
Continue the use of the subsidiary ledger/schedule per Barangay; and
Thereafter, conduct periodic reconciliation of records with the CTO for fair presentation of account balances.
The City Treasurer’s Office to:
Provide a complete list of taxpayers and the corresponding amount of uncollected delinquent taxes to the CAO necessary for the establishment of the subsidiary ledgers;
Provide accurate and reliable amount of RPT/SET collectibles at the beginning of each year in accordance with Section 20 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (NGAS); and
Continue undertaking periodic reconciliation of records with the City Accounting Office to enable adjustments in the appropriate records for any noted discrepancies.

Recommendation/s:
We recommended to the management to:
Expedite the investigation of PPE items not located/found, non-existent and missing in the RPCPPE and follow the disposition procedures prescribed in Sections 7 and 8 of COA Circular No. 2020-006;
Instruct the City Accounting and General Services Offices to investigate and adjust accordingly the identified PPE items recorded twice in the books as presented in Table 4;
Instruct the Inventory Committee to:
ensure that complete PPE items are subjected to physical count and
finalize and submit the Inventory Count Form, List of PPEs Found at Station and List of Non/Existing and Missing PPEs; and
Include in the annual physical count the Work/Zoo Animals and the CIP accounts to establish their existence and the correctness of the reported balances.

Recommendation/s:
We recommended the Management to direct the City Accountant to:
Review and rectify the manually computed depreciation schedule to enable the accurate verification of the system generated depreciation and correctly adjust discrepancies, if any;
Draw up a JEV to adjust the CY 2023 accumulated depreciation after review and correction of the manual depreciation schedule; and
Constantly review and monitor the system-generated depreciation schedule for the PPE and evaluate the correctness of the data using the underlying provisions and basis of computation provided by IPSAS 17, and other generally accepted accounting principles (GAAP).

Recommendation/s:
We recommended to the City Mayor to instruct the City Accounting Office to:
Strictly ensure compliance with IPSAS 23 in relation to revenue from non- exchange transactions, specifically on the fund transfers, taking into consideration the terms and conditions in the MOA and ensure that revenue is recognized equivalent to the liability reduced in view of the fulfillment of conditions relative to the funds recorded in the Trust Fund.
Promptly transfer all completed projects from the Trust Fund to the General Fund to ensure that depreciation expense is recognized in the proper books.
Promptly transfer the net revenue arising from interest on bank to the General Fund for proper recognition of income.

Recommendation/s:
We recommended to the management to:
Instruct the City Accounting and Budget Office to identify the causes of the discrepancy aggregating P21,510,429.14 and submit the said reconciliation to COA.
Reconcile the variance of P580,299.29 between the Special Trust Fund balance in the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF with the TL- DRRMF account and make the necessary adjustments and corrections in the respective records/reports.
Instruct the concerned offices to scrutinize the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRM Fund and require periodic and timely reconciliation with accounting and budget records to ensure that errors are corrected on a timely manner, to reflect accurate balances at any given reporting period and to correctly make the necessary disclosures in the financial statements.

Recommendation/s:
We recommended to the management to direct the LDRRMC, thru the LDRRM Office, to prepare the LDRRMFIP/LDRRMP in accordance with the prescribed format, attached herewith and annually prepare the LDRRMFIP/LDRRMP for the Regular Annual and Supplemental Budgets and include therein, in a separate caption, the projects and activities to be charged to the unexpended LDRRMF in the Special Trust Fund.

Recommendation/s:
We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations to the management to:
Maximize the utilization of current and prior year’s appropriations of the 20% development fund. Exhaustive implementation should be undertaken by management, through extensive planning and monitoring and immediate resolution of identified issues of the remaining unimplemented 2001-2023 PPAs, to cope with the subsequent year PPA’s that equally needs prompt implementation.
Require the City Development Council and the budget committee during the preparation of the annual budget for the 20% Development Fund to include only Programs/Projects/Activities which are implementation ready and could be accomplished within the target date of completion, after taking into consideration the priorities of the LGU leadership;
Require the City Planning and Development Coordinator (CPDC) to:
Closely and intensively monitor and evaluate the implementation of the different development programs, projects and activities identified in the approved development plan as mandated by Section 476 (4) of R. A. 7160 to address and resolve timely any implementation issues that may arise;
Re-evaluate the programs/projects under the continuing appropriations and identify projects which are determined to still address the present development needs of the intended beneficiaries and execute them without delay, otherwise, facilitate its reversion and re-appropriation to fund other crucial development projects and programs of the city.
Ensure that all savings from completed projects and all projects that are no longer feasible and not yet implemented are considered for immediate realignment, subject to budgetary rules, or reverted back to the unappropriated surplus of the 20% Development Fund for re-appropriation to other development projects.

Recommendation/s:
We recommended to the management to direct the City Officials to adopt a system and a more proactive approach in deciding how future donated funds will be utilized in order to ultimately achieve the sensible purpose of the funds received.

Recommendation/s:
We recommended to the management to direct the responsible officials to submit the noted deficient supporting documents and henceforth, ensure that all payments of terminal leave benefits and disbursement of government funds in general are supported with the required documentary requirements as enumerated under COA Circular No. 2023-004, CSC Memorandum Circular No. 06, s. of 2003 and other applicable rules and regulations.

Recommendation/s:
We recommended to the management to direct the City Accounting Office to:
Undertake a thorough review and analysis of the details of the mandatory Inter-Agency accounts and submit to the audit team the reconciliation of amounts withheld still existing as of December 31, 2023;
Facilitate reconciliation with the concerned government agencies if necessary; and
If warranted, cause immediate remittance to various government agencies the amount due for the current and past due payables not yet remitted.

We recommended to the City Accounting Office and City General Services Office to expedite the reconciliation of balances of the PPE accounts in view of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020. Require both Offices to submit a valid justification for the delayed reconciliation contrary to the time prescribed in the Circular. Also, require both Offices to submit and commit to a time frame in which the reconciliation and one-time cleansing of accounts should be finished.

We recommended to the Head of the Agency to require the CAO to abide with existing reporting rules and regulations. Henceforth, present separately the Subisdies to NGA and other LGUs in the line item “Transfers, Assistance, and Subsidy To” in the face of the F/S

We recommended to the Head of the Agency to require the CAO to:
Reconcile the items recorded in the accounts and effect adjustments, if any;
Submit the reconciliation and journal entry voucher for the adjustments to the Office of the Auditor for verification; and
Henceforth, ensure due diligence in the recording of reciprocal accounts and conduct regular reconciliation of the accounts to timely address any variances.

We recommended to the Head of the Agency to submit written justification for the above disbursement and submit legal basis or bases to support the same. Henceforth, exercise prudence in the disbursement of government funds. Discontinue granting similar allowances, incentives, and benefits to individuals in the absence of any legal or statutory basis.

We recommended to the Head of the Agency to submit written justification for the above disbursement and submit legal basis or bases to support the same. Henceforth, exercise prudence in the disbursement of government funds. Discontinue granting similar allowances, incentives, and benefits to individuals in the absence of any legal or statutory basis, especially to those employed without an employee-employer relationship.

We recommended to the management to require the accountable officers to:
Ensure that the cashbook is maintained in accordance with Section 27, Chapter 3, Volume II of NGAS – Local. Require the accountable officers to accomplish the required monthly certifications, and
Enhance the cash book in accordance with the prescribed format in the LTOM and ensure that reference numbers are provided to ensure efficient review of the collections reported.

We recommended to the Head of the Agency to instruct the BAC to thoroughly review the documents submitted by the bidder to ensure that it conforms to the Manual of Procedures for the Procurement of Goods and Services, the PBD, and GPPB, in line with R.A. 9184. Submit a justification with detailed computation on the reasonableness of the P2,180,436.27 variance. Likewise, submit a valid justification on the failure to perform appropriate action on the difference between the bid price as calculated and bid price in the bid form.

We recommended to the Head of the Agency to require the end-user to strictly comply with Section I, Annex “H” of the Revised IRR of RA 9184. Instead of specifying the brand name, ensure that the specifications in the PR shall be detailed but not tailor-fitted, and be based on the relevant characteristics, performance requirements, and/or features to ensure value for money and prevent legal challenges.

We recommended to the Head of the Agency the following:
Revise the existing MOA to specifically state therein the coverage of the funeral services and the fixed cost involved;
Require the CSWD to conduct a case study or equivalent on the families granted with free cost of funeral services. Determine appropriateness of the grant and require the reimbursement of the cost of funeral services for those who are unqualified.
Require the CDRRMO to submit the death certificates as well as the barangay, city health and/or hospital certification stating therewith that the deceased was a suspected/confirmed COVID-19 patient.
Henceforth, ensure compliance to existing laws, rules, and regulations pertaining the payment of the cost of funeral services in relation to the COVID-19 pandemic.

We recommended to the Head of the Agency to instruct the City Accountant and the City Budget Office to strictly comply with the existing rules and regulations pertaining the recording of CNA Incentive.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:

The management shall:

a.1 Ensure that all disbursements charged against 20% Development fund (20%DF) partake the nature of investment or capital expenditure. 

a.2 Review the LGU’s development problems, issues, and goals embodied in the Comprehensive Development Plan (CDP) and 5-Year Local Development Investment Plan (LDIP), improve strategies to incorporate them in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) and Annual Budgets, and implement these development projects effectively and efficiently to truly develop/improve the living condition of the constituents in the LGU. 

The City Accountant shall refer to the Annual Budgets regarding the classification of the expenditures (whether MOOE or CO) to supplement the evaluation of the nature of the expenditures and subsequently determine the appropriate accounting entry in accordance with IPSAS.

The City Budget Officer shall enhance the review of budget especially the items under 20% development fund that it should partake the nature of capital expenditures and fall within the enumerated allowable development projects under DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-001.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:

a.	the management shall exert further efforts to locate and contact the developer/owner of Azura Subdivision and to negotiate for the donation of the road lots, sidewalks, etc. in the same private subdivision. 

b. if ownership of the road lots has been transferred in the name of the City, the management shall recognize in the books of the accounts the fair market value of the road lots as at the date of acquisition pursuant to IPSAS 23.

c. if proof of ownership is absent, the management shall refrain from implementing road concreting; otherwise, expenses incurred in it shall be disallowed in audit, for it being considered illegal expenditure under Annex “B” of COA Circular 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012. Legal remedy shall be initiated for the recovery or reimbursement of the cost or expenses it incurred in improving or concreting the road lots, sidewalks, etc. The persons determined to be liable shall be required to refund the amount disbursed.
observations.managementAction: 2019 AAR | Management has exerted efforts to locate the housing developer of Azura Subdivision, however, the same could not be located and no further information of the same was acquired.
observations.reason:
The road lots, sidewalks were not donated to the City Government of Malaybalay as of December 31, 2021, hence, the same was not recognized in the books of accounts of the City.

Review of the disbursement in CY 2021 disclosed no additional payment for the same.

As for its justification that the developer could not be located, no proof that at least a written communication was mailed to the said developer.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to submit justification as to why the subject land herein was not registered under the Torrens Title System. Also, require the following Offices to:

A. General Services Office:

•Verify and secure copies of Deed of donation and other proof of ownership with these land in order to register such by exhausting all possible means.

•Register all unregistered land under the Torrens Title System with the Local Registration Authority – Registry of Deeds, in accordance with the provisions of Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386;

•Ensure custody of all land titles of the City by establishing an archival system to ensure the safety and protection of all government property, public documents or records such as property inventory report, land ownership, and such other records or documents of public interest in the various offices and departments in the municipality.

B. City Accounting Office:

•Coordinate with the City General Services Office as to the unrecorded land and effect necessary adjustment in the books of accounts, should documents found available warrants recording of the herein land in the books of accounts.

C. City Assessors Office:

•Communicate with the Land Registration Authority/Registry of Deeds and secure a copy of the list of lands or titles, if possible, belonging to the City, and reconcile results thereof with the Land recorded in the books of accounts and in the ledger card maintained by the General Services Office; Investigate discrepancies, if any, and communicate with the Accounting Office and General Services Office noted discrepancies to facilitate adjustments in the books of accounts and/or ledger cards.
observations.managementAction:
2019 AAR | We quote: "Out of the forty three (43) parcels of land in the summarized list, only eight (8) are declared in the name of the City Government of Malaybalay and thirty five (35) parcels of land are erroneously submitted by us to your office that should have been appropriately recorded as Land in the Books of Accounts of the respective barangay where it was declared.

Per initial research of the City Legal Office and the Office of the City Assessor the records of the 6 may have been lost, only one (1) property in the list tagged as Item No. 7 (TD No. F-002131) or the 6,578 square meters, more or less, parcel property where the old city hall site is located was retrieved, as per copy attached Deed of Sale sometime in 1950 and Transfer Certificate of Title (TCT) No. T-1116.

The other six (6) properties are alienable parcels of land (may have been old LRC issuances) that needs to be further researched/retrieved in the archive of the Land Management System (LMS) of the Department of Environment and Natural Resources (DENR) Region 10 in Cagayan de Oro City, before we can commence retrieval at the Registry of Deeds of the Province of Bukidnon (if already registered) and/or petition for its reissuance sans available records at the DENR Provincial Office. The remaining property in Barangay Manalog (Item No. 29/TD No. F-030142) is still classified as inalienable public land (public forest), hence the said property still cannot be titled.

However, despite loss of our records, we are mindful of your recommendations, we shall record the 6 parcel of land to the Books of Accounts of the City Government pending retrieval, and have them surveyed and registered if they are still not, as soon as we have the necessary datus from the LMS/DENR Region 10."
observations.reason: Only 6 out of 43 are proper land properties owned by the City, however, the same was not yet recorded at year end due to the lack of necessary documents from LMS/DENR Region 10.

observations.recommendation:
It was recommended to management that, henceforth, the City Accountant shall be required to issue to the City Mayor or any other authorized officer entering into contract or obligation in behalf of the City Government a certification that funds have been duly appropriated for and the amount necessary to cover the proposed contract for the current year is available for expenditure on account thereof. The certificate signed by the proper accounting official shall be attached to and become an integral part of the proposed contract. The sum so certified shall not thereafter be available for expenditure for any other purpose until the obligation of the city government under the contract is fully extinguished (c.f. Section 86, Chapter 4, Title II, PD 1445).

It was recommended further that management shall come up with sound agreements with and policies for the use of the shared working space by private entities and individuals such that they may partly share the costs of operating the ICT Common Working Space.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The City Engineering Office to carefully examine the Program of Works to avoid future variation orders, extra works so as to avoid delays in the implementation of infrastructure projects. 
The City Planning Office should look into the extra work orders being attached to the duly approved program of works to ensure that the same is within its AIP and that the delivery of its basic services to its constituents/end users be rightfully served on time. Henceforth, 
The City Accountant is recommended to impose liquidated damages to its erring suppliers and contractors in the formula specified in the revised IRR of RA 9184 to minimize government loss. Also, 
The BAC, more particularly, the Technical Working Group, shall validate the list of completed and on-going projects undertaken by the bidder and required the attachment of the final acceptance certificate to validate contractors performance and be a factor of its criteria in its post- qualifications in the bidding transactions of its agency.
observations.reason: Suspension of works but the said projects could no longer be verified in the City's Project Status Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Head of the Agency to instruct the City Accountant and the City Budget Office to strictly comply with the existing rules and regulations pertaining the recording of CNA Incentive.

We recommend to the Head of the Agency to commend the efforts of the CGSO and the City Accounting for initiating the compliance to the one-time cleansing of accounts under COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
Nonetheless, after compliance of the above-mentioned Circular, ensure continuous reconciliation of ledger and property cards maintained by the City Accounting and the CGSO. Strictly require the periodic physical count of the properties and ensure that the required Report of Physical Count of PPEs is submitted to expedite effective and efficient verification of the existence of the properties reported. Moreover, ensure that all records supporting the ownership and disposition of the properties is kept by the CGSO.

We recommended to the Management to require the City Accounting Office to:
Reconcile the items recorded in the accounts and to effect adjustments, if any;
Submit the reconciliation and journal entry voucher for the adjustments to the Office of the Auditor for verification; and
Conduct regular reconciliation of the accounts to eliminate difference.

We recommended to the Head of the Agency to instruct the City Accounting Office and the City Budget Office together with the Employees’ Organization-Management Consultative Committee to strictly comply with the requirements of the existing rules and regulations pertaining the grant of CNA Incentive, particularly COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and DBM Budget Circular No. 2020-5 dated November 4, 2020. Thus, require the said Offices to submit the following documents to support the validity of the claim:
Proof of transaction with LandBank with summary as to the names and bank accounts credited;
Detailed computation of unencumbered allowable MOOE allotments after considering FY 2020 requirements, certified by the head of the agency.
Accomplishment report showing that at least an average of 70% of all agency targets have been accomplished

We recommended to the City Mayor to direct the City Treasurer through the City Accountant to submit to the COA the aforementioned 709 cancelled checks for validation, otherwise, secure a stop payment order of the 709 checks duly acknowledged by the bank and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor. The bank charges of the said stop payment order shall not be charged to the expenses of the LGU.
Henceforth, ensure that all cancelled checks are attached to the RCI and reported chronologically with the other checks issued with the word “Cancelled” clearly indicated therein.

We recommended to the Head of the Agency to require the BAC to submit a justification why price data from the DA, DTI, and the City Government of Malaybalay’s website was not used in determining the budget cost for each relief. Also, submit a computation and documents showing which price data is used and how the budget for the individual items in the aforementioned relief packs were computed.
Henceforth, improve the City’s procurement procedure by ensuring that the budget cost in the procurement of goods is anchored from the data available from the implementing agencies enumerated in Section 3(3) of RA 7581 and strictly comply with Section 5 of GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020.

We recommended to the management:
to require the concerned City Officials for the immediate submission of certified list of beneficiaries and complete Relief Distribution Sheets / Food Assistance Distribution Sheets as proof that all available food relief goods were actually and fairly received by intended families under RA No. 11469
to justify variances noted in the distribution sheets for distribution of relief goods from Bayanihan funds and Unspent LDRRM funds and cash assistance of PGB for social amelioration of 217,500 total beneficiaries in the LGU
to provide written justification of the inconsistencies and irregularities in the distribution of cash assistance from PGB and ensure future donations and assistance from national and local agencies provides Memorandum of Agreement for proper disbursement and liquidation of funds

We recommended the following:
The management shall
a.1. Justify the P170,010.00 expenses inappropriately charged in BGCM with supporting documents, otherwise, furnish the Auditor the certified true copy of the OR for the refund.
a.2 Ensure that all disbursements charged against BGCM shall partake the 
COVID-19-related programs, projects, and activities (PPAs) incorporated in LBC No. 125.
a.3 Review the LGU’s COVID-19 assessment problems and issues and counter such by implementing projects and activities effectively and efficiently to truly develop/improve the safety condition of the constituents in the LGU.
The LFC shall
b.1 Review guidelines on LBC 125 to boost its capacity in immediately responding to the COVID-19 emergency by providing projects and activities in line with the Circular.
b.2  Reconsider options that are not in the guidelines which may be a hassle on the part of work in-charge specially for the City Treasurer’s cash book maintenance of its separate bank account and another Bank Reconciliation Statement preparation on the part of the City Accountant for opening a new bank account.
b.3  Direct the DILG in-charge of the LGU for full disclosure of utilization and status if implementation of PPAs for BGCM for transparency and accountability

We recommended the following:
The management shall:
a.1 Ensure that all disbursements charged against 20% Development fund (20%DF) partake the nature of investment or capital expenditure.
a.2 Review the LGU’s development problems, issues, and goals embodied in the Comprehensive Development Plan (CDP) and 5-Year Local Development Investment Plan (LDIP), improve strategies to incorporate them in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) and Annual Budgets, and implement these development projects effectively and efficiently to truly develop/improve the living condition of the constituents in the LGU.
The City Accountant shall refer to the Annual Budgets regarding the classification of the expenditures (whether MOOE or CO) to supplement the evaluation of the nature of the expenditures and subsequently determine the appropriate accounting entry in accordance with IPSAS.
The City Budget Officer shall enhance the review of budget especially the items under 20% development fund that it should partake the nature of capital expenditures and fall within the enumerated allowable development projects under DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-001.

We recommended to the Head of the Agency to require the City Budget Office together with the local sanggunian to submit a joint justification with regards to the herein observation. Moreover, advise the local sanggunian to strictly comply with the provisions of Section 21 of RA 10121 and judiciously determine and ensure that, henceforth, reverted unutilized LDRRMF should be duly appropriated for social service purposes only.

We recommended the following:

a.	the management shall exert further efforts to locate and contact the developer/owner of Azura Subdivision and to negotiate for the donation of the road lots, sidewalks, etc. in the same private subdivision. 

b. if ownership of the road lots has been transferred in the name of the City, the management shall recognize in the books of the accounts the fair market value of the road lots as at the date of acquisition pursuant to IPSAS 23.

c. if proof of ownership is absent, the management shall refrain from implementing road concreting; otherwise, expenses incurred in it shall be disallowed in audit, for it being considered illegal expenditure under Annex “B” of COA Circular 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012. Legal remedy shall be initiated for the recovery or reimbursement of the cost or expenses it incurred in improving or concreting the road lots, sidewalks, etc. The persons determined to be liable shall be required to refund the amount disbursed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to submit justification as to why the subject land herein was not registered under the Torrens Title System. Also, require the following Offices to:

	A. General Services Office:

•Verify and secure copies of Deed of donation and other proof of ownership with these land in order to register such by exhausting all possible means.

•Register all unregistered land under the Torrens Title System with the Local Registration Authority – Registry of Deeds, in accordance with the provisions of Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386;

•Ensure custody of all land titles of the City by establishing an archival system to ensure the safety and protection of all government property, public documents or records such as property inventory report, land ownership, and such other records or documents of public interest in the various offices and departments in the municipality.
B. City Accounting Office:

•Coordinate with the City General Services Office as to the unrecorded land and effect necessary adjustment in the books of accounts, should documents found available warrants recording of the herein land in the books of accounts.

C. City Assessors Office:

•Communicate with the Land Registration Authority/Registry of Deeds and secure a copy of the list of lands or titles, if possible, belonging to the City, and reconcile results thereof with the Land recorded in the books of accounts and in the ledger card maintained by the General Services Office; Investigate discrepancies, if any, and communicate with the Accounting Office and General Services Office noted discrepancies to facilitate adjustments in the books of accounts and/or ledger cards.
observations.managementAction:
We quote: "Out of the forty three (43) parcels of land in the summarized list, only eight (8) are declared in the name of the City Government of Malaybalay and thirty five (35) parcels of land are erroneously submitted by us to your office that should have been appropriately recorded as Land in the Books of Accounts of the respective barangay where it was declared.

Per initial research of the City Legal Office and the Office of the City Assessor the records of the 6 may have been lost, only one (1) property in the list tagged as Item No. 7 (TD No. F-002131) or the 6,578 square meters, more or less, parcel property where the old city hall site is located was retrieved, as per copy attached Deed of Sale sometime in 1950 and Transfer Certificate of Title (TCT) No. T-1116.

The other six (6) properties are alienable parcels of land (may have been old LRC issuances) that needs to be further researched/retrieved in the archive of the Land Management System (LMS) of the Department of Environment and Natural Resources (DENR) Region 10 in Cagayan de Oro City, before we can commence retrieval at the Registry of Deeds of the Province of Bukidnon (if already registered) and/or petition for its reissuance sans available records at the DENR Provincial Office. The remaining property in Barangay Manalog (Item No. 29/TD No. F-030142) is still classified as inalienable public land (public forest), hence the said property still cannot be titled.

However, despite loss of our records, we are mindful of your recommendations, we shall record the 6 parcel of land to the Books of Accounts of the City Government pending retrieval, and have them surveyed and registered if they are still not, as soon as we have the necessary datus from the LMS/DENR Region 10."
observations.reason: Only 6 out of 43 are proper land properties owned by the City, however, the same was not yet recorded at year end due to the lack of necessary documents from LMS/DENR Region 10.
